name: Nunet
description: Nunet, also spelled Nounet, is the female counterpart to Nu, representing the primordial waters of chaos and the absence of form from which the world was created in ancient Egyptian mythology. She is one of the Ogdoad, a group of eight primordial deities worshipped in Hermopolis. Nunet embodies the watery abyss that existed before the creation of the cosmos, a boundless and undifferentiated state of potential. She is typically depicted as a woman with blue or green skin, symbolizing the waters she embodies, and often with the head of a snake or frog, representing the primordial nature of her being. Alongside her male counterpart, Nu, she represents the passive, formless aspect of creation, the unmanifested potential that precedes existence. Nunet's role is crucial in the cyclical nature of existence in Egyptian cosmology. After the world's destruction, it is believed that everything returns to the primordial waters of Nun and Nunet, awaiting a new cycle of creation. She possesses immense power, as she is the source from which all things originate and to which all things eventually return. She is not actively worshipped as a central deity but is revered as a fundamental aspect of the universe's creation and destruction, a constant reminder of the boundless potential that lies beneath the surface of reality. Her essence is the very fabric of the cosmos before it takes shape, a silent, powerful force that underpins all of existence.
subtitle: Primordial Waters of Creation

keyMyths.title: The Creation from Nun
keyMyths.description: The world emerges from the primordial waters of Nun and Nunet, representing the transition from formlessness to form. It is a process of differentiation and order arising from chaos.

keyMyths.title: The Ogdoad's Role
keyMyths.description: Nunet, as part of the Ogdoad, helps maintain the balance of the cosmos before creation. She represents the female aspect of the primordial waters, balancing Nu's male aspect.

keyMyths.title: Return to the Waters
keyMyths.description: At the end of a cosmic cycle, it is believed that everything will return to the primordial waters of Nun and Nunet. This signifies a return to potential before a new creation.
